---
title: "Chapter 12 - Bhakti Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">भक्तियोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-11.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-13.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-12-1" data-reference="12.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-1">12.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those devotees who, ever steadfast, worship you, and those who worship the imperishable and unmanifest—which of them better know <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एवं सततयुक्ता ये भक्तास्त्वां पर्युपासते ।<br>
ये चाप्यक्षरमव्यक्तं तेषां के योगवित्तमाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-2" data-reference="12.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-2">12.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those who fix their minds on me and, ever steadfast, worship me with supreme faith—them I hold to be the most perfect in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मय्यावेश्य मनो ये मां नित्ययुक्ता उपासते ।<br>
श्रद्धया परयोपेताः ते मे युक्ततमा मताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-3" data-reference="12.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-3">12.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But those who worship the imperishable, the undefinable, the unmanifest, all-pervading and unthinkable, unchanging, fixed, and still—</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ये त्वक्षरमनिर्देश्यमव्यक्तं पर्युपासते ।<br>
सर्वत्रगमचिन्त्यञ्च कूटस्थमचलन्ध्रुवम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-4" data-reference="12.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-4">12.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">having restrained the whole set of senses, even-minded in all circumstances, rejoicing in the welfare of all beings—they too attain me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सन्नियम्येन्द्रियग्रामं सर्वत्र समबुद्धयः ।<br>
ते प्राप्नुवन्ति मामेव सर्वभूतहिते रताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-5" data-reference="12.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-5">12.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Greater is the toil of those whose minds are set on the unmanifest; for the unmanifest goal is hard for the embodied to reach.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">क्लेशोऽधिकतरस्तेषामव्यक्तासक्तचेतसाम् ।<br>
अव्यक्ता हि गतिर्दुःखं देहवद्भिरवाप्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-6" data-reference="12.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-6">12.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But those who, surrendering all actions to me, intent on me, worship me, meditating with undivided <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ये तु सर्वाणि कर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्पराः ।<br>
अनन्येनैव योगेन मां ध्यायन्त उपासते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-7" data-reference="12.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-7">12.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For them, whose minds are absorbed in me, I soon become the deliverer from the ocean of death and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="samsara: The ongoing cycle of worldly becoming, birth, death, and attachment." aria-label="samsara: The ongoing cycle of worldly becoming, birth, death, and attachment." title="samsara: The ongoing cycle of worldly becoming, birth, death, and attachment." data-glossary-id="samsara">samsara</span>, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तेषामहं समुद्धर्ता मृत्युसंसारसागरात् ।<br>
भवामि नचिरात्पार्थ मय्यावेशितचेतसाम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-8" data-reference="12.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-8">12.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Fix your mind on me alone, rest your understanding in me; thereafter you will dwell in me alone—of this there is no doubt.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मय्येव मन आधत्स्व मयि बुद्धिं निवेशय ।<br>
निवसिष्यसि मय्येव अत ऊर्ध्वं न संशयः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-9" data-reference="12.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-9">12.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But if you cannot fix your mind steadily on me, then seek to reach me by the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of practice, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." aria-label="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." title="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." data-glossary-id="dhananjaya">Dhananjaya</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अथ चित्तं समाधातुं न शक्नोषि मयि स्थिरम् ।<br>
अभ्यासयोगेन ततो मामिच्छाप्तुं धनञ्जय ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-10" data-reference="12.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-10">12.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">If you are unable even to practice, be intent on working for me; doing your actions for my sake, you too will attain perfection.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अभ्यासेऽप्यसमर्थोऽसि मत्कर्मपरमो भव ।<br>
मदर्थमपि कर्माणि कुर्वन्सिद्धिमवाप्स्यसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-11" data-reference="12.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-11">12.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And if even this you cannot do, then, taking refuge in my <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, with self subdued, give up the fruit of all action.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अथैतदप्यशक्तोऽसि कर्तुं मद्योगमाश्रितः ।<br>
सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं ततः कुरु यतात्मवान् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-12" data-reference="12.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-12">12.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Knowledge is better than practice; meditation surpasses knowledge; better than meditation is the renouncing of the fruits of action—from such renouncing, peace follows at once.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">श्रेयो हि ज्ञानमभ्यासाज्ज्ञानाद्ध्यानं विशिष्यते ।<br>
ध्यानात्कर्मफलत्यागस्त्यागाच्छान्तिरनन्तरम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-13" data-reference="12.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-13">12.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who hates no being, who is friendly and compassionate, free of possessiveness and ego, even in pain and pleasure, and forgiving;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च ।<br>
निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-14" data-reference="12.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-14">12.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">ever content, the yogin self-controlled, of firm resolve, his mind and understanding given to me—he who is devoted to me is dear to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः ।<br>
मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-15" data-reference="12.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-15">12.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He from whom the world is not disturbed, and who is not disturbed by the world, who is free from joy, resentment, fear, and agitation—he is dear to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः ।<br>
हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-16" data-reference="12.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-16">12.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who is not dependent on anything, pure, capable, impartial, and untroubled, who has renounced all undertakings—that devotee of mine is dear to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः ।<br>
सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-17" data-reference="12.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-17">12.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who neither rejoices nor hates, neither grieves nor longs, who has given up concern with good and bad outcomes—that devotee of mine is dear to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ्क्षति ।<br>
शुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्तिमान्यः स मे प्रियः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-18" data-reference="12.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-18">12.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The same to foe and friend, the same in honor and dishonor, the same in cold and heat, in pleasure and pain, free of attachment;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः ।<br>
शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-19" data-reference="12.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-19">12.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">weighing blame and praise alike, silent, content with whatever comes, without a fixed home, steady of mind, full of devotion—such a person is dear to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येन केनचित् ।<br>
अनिकेतः स्थिरमतिर्भक्तिमान्मे प्रियो नरः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-20" data-reference="12.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-20">12.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But those who honor this nectar of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span>, as I have told it, full of faith and holding me as the highest—those devotees are exceedingly dear to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ये तु धर्म्यामृतमिदं यथोक्तं पर्युपासते ।<br>
श्रद्दधाना मत्परमा भक्तास्तेऽतीव मे प्रियाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
